archimate:TechnologyService.name: Database Cache Manager
archimate:TechnologyService.documentation:
Definition: Database Cache Manager ABB is a Technology Service that implements the behaviour of a software system that manages the caching of frequently accessed data in memory
Source: Inspired in the definition provided by relebant database vendors
Example: The following implementation is an example on how this specific Architecture Building Block (ABB) can be instantiated as a Solution Building Block (SBB): Redis, The open source, in-memory data store used by millions of developers as a database, cache, streaming engine, and message broker. https://redis.io/
Interoperability Dimension: Structural IoP
LOST view: Technical view - infrastructure
Interoperability Saliency: Database Cache Manager ABB is salient for semantic interoperability due to implementing a mechanism to manage data in memory to provide to the public administration consumers efficiently and in a short time response. It also facilitates data sharing and reuse using common data exchange models and serialisations.
Identifier: http://data.europa.eu/dr8/DatabaseCacheManagerTechnologyService
ABB name: eira:DatabaseCacheManagerTechnologyService
EIRA concept: eira:ArchitectureBuildingBlock
Last modification: 2024-01-28
Identifier: http://data.europa.eu/dr8/DatabaseCacheManagerTechnologyService
Interoperability Layer: TechnicalInfrastructure

properties.value: Database Cache Manager ABB is a Technology Service that implements the behaviour of a software system that manages the caching of frequently accessed data in memory
Inspired in the definition provided by relebant database vendors

The following implementation is an example on how this specific Architecture Building Block (ABB) can be instantiated as a Solution Building Block (SBB): Redis, The open source, in-memory data store used by millions of developers as a database, cache, streaming engine, and message broker. https://redis.io/

Database Cache Manager ABB is salient for semantic interoperability due to implementing a mechanism to manage data in memory to provide to the public administration consumers efficiently and in a short time response. It also facilitates data sharing and reuse using common data exchange models and serialisations.
